content.problemStatement:
Alice buys a toy with a selling price of 100100100 rupees. There is a discount of xxx percent on the toy. Find the amount Alice needs to pay for it.


Input Format

The first line of input will contain a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases.
The first and only line of each test case contains a single integer, xxx — the discount on the toy.


Output Format
For each test case, output on a new line the price that Alice needs to pay.
content.inputFormat: The first line contains an integer T, the number of test cases. Each of the next T lines contains an integer x, the discount percentage (0 ≤ x < 100).
content.outputFormat: For each test case, output a single line with the amount Alice has to pay after applying the discount.

sampleTestCases.explanation: For each discount x, the payable amount is 100 - x. Hence: 100-5=95, 100-9=91, 100-11=89, 100-21=79.
No discount, so Alice pays the full price of 100 rupees.
A 99% discount reduces the price to 100-99=1 rupee.

solution.approach: The original price of the toy is fixed at 100 rupees. For each test case we read the discount percentage x and compute the payable amount as 100 - x. This is a direct O(1) arithmetic operation per test case.
solution.timeComplexity: O(T) – one constant‑time computation for each of the T test cases.
solution.spaceComplexity: O(1) – only a few integer variables are used; output storage is not counted.
